old_claim.note: GPS-Guided Logistics provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct GPS-guided logistics dependency remains because autonomous aircraft navigation, guidance, control, onboard computing, and mission execution do not causally require a commercial fleet-logistics workflow.

source_shape.source_locator: Unmanned Aircraft Systems page: describes autonomous mission execution through onboard navigation, guidance, control, planning, perception, and computing, including operation in GPS-denied environments.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: JPL documents drone autonomy as an onboard robotic navigation and control capability that can function without GPS availability.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Even when an autonomous aircraft uses GPS, that sensor input is distinct from the downstream business process of fleet tracking, routing, dispatch, and delivery management.

ontology_before: Drone autonomy was modeled as directly enabled by a later commercial GPS fleet-logistics application.
ontology_after: Drone autonomy retains its mobile-robot navigation lineage without importing an unrelated logistics workflow as a prerequisite.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-16-gps-navstar-logistics-boundary: drone_autonomy has no direct gps_guided_logistics prerequisite while mobile_robot_navigation remains present.
would_reject_if: The node is rescoped specifically to autonomous logistics-drone fleet dispatch rather than general drone autonomy.
Authoritative architecture evidence makes commercial GPS fleet-management software a constituent of autonomous aircraft navigation and control.
short_reason: Drone autonomy is an onboard navigation-and-control capability, not a derivative of GPS fleet logistics.
